title: Monday + Third Party Email Platform Integration
excerpt:

posts.author: scottjberman (Scott Berman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello -

There are other discussions about integrations with other third party email providers, like MailChimp (see thread here and how the integrations are very lacking. I explore integrating with MailChimp myself and found it very weak (you can basically only pass through email address, which makes it useless).

I looked into some other email platforms, like MailerLite, and while some things can be done with Zapier to make it more usable, the sheer number of Zaps needed and the complexity of how we are using Monday as a CRM makes that route impractical… and the system would be bound to break where we’d have automations in Monday, many Zaps in Zapier, and many automations in MailerLite, for example.

Has anyone found a practical solution for integrating Monday and a third-party email platform?

Also, I know Monday has some email functionality, and we’ll use some of it, but we still need a third party platform.

Any thoughts are appreciated!
Scott

posts.author: Ilan.A (Ilan Axelrod)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Scott

We can connect Mailchimp to your Monday account using an API.
If you’ve got questions . You can reach me at:
ilan@formrally.com.

Thanks,
Ilan

posts.author: waterknot (Josh Nusbau)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Scott,

Curious if you got anywhere with this. I have the same issue and have made zero progress.

We already used Klaviyo for email marketing because it has some specific functionality that MailChimp doesn’t have (plus, as you mention, the MC/Monday integration is near useless). I got sold on using Zapier to integrate Monday with Klaviyo. I put in a lot of time buildign zaps. My integrations tested perfectly but then never actually worked in real life. I got nowhere with Monday or Zapier support (each pointed me to the other for help).

Long story short, I am still wondering if there is a truly useful Monday/eMail marketing platform integration solution.

Thanks,
Josh

posts.author: smit (Smit Golakiya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @scottjberman

You can use EasyMails app, you can use drag and drop template designer, easily upload files directly from your board column, and seamlessly integrate your board data into your email content.

It’s like having MailService inside monday .com, You can use SMTP of different platform to Connect.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @scottjberman, @waterknot

We developed an app called Mailchimp Integration Plus that provides a robust integration with Mailchimp. Amongst other capabilities, it allows to do full mapping of fields from monday to Mailchimp, including custom fields for merge tags. Here’s an example →

Screenshot 2024-05-13 at 12.17.122714×1440 269 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - monday.com: Apps Marketplace

Hope that helps!

posts.author: PayWay369 (paytonwest@hotmail.com)
posts.cookedHtml: will this provide the ability to automatically send a “daily progress report” to an external client? example: we have a board for each project where our foremans enters the teams daily progress and each day i want to automatically send the exact updates to our clients. for the life of me, i dont understand why monday doesnt have this feature…its basic software development.

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Scott Barman

Welcome to the Monday.com Communit

Integration of Monday.com with a third-party email platform,

We can set up Make.com to create seamless workflows that bridge Monday.com and your preferred email service. This solution minimizes reliance on numerous Zaps and prevents potential automation breakdowns by centralizing the automation process.
lIke
Mapping out essential email data points between Monday.com and the chosen email service.
Setting up automated workflows in Make.com to handle data transfer, ensuring all necessary information is synced in real-time.
Testing and monitoring the workflows to ensure reliability and efficiency.

posts.author: jamespatten (James Patten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Scott,

Some great options already posted above, if you’re still looking for another solution on this, you can now integrate Mailchimp into monday using the Data Importer monday app.

A few things you can do with Mailchimp using Data Importer:

  • Bulk import your entire contact list from an audience.
  • Import various contact fields (e.g. name, email, status, custom merge fields and more) plus stats (e.g. average open rate)
  • Schedule imports to keep your monday board in sync when new contacts are added to Mailchimp.

I’ve also created a step-by-step tutorial for more details on how to import Mailchimp contacts

Note this is only for pulling mailchimp contacts into monday, not sending emails from monday. Happy to answer any questions on this.

Monday Mailchimp Integration1992×1084 141 KB

posts.author: Mike_C (Michael Collins)
posts.cookedHtml: Some good replies here, however, working in a government funded space, we need to store our data in Australia. Looked into Zapier, but they store their data in US; does anyone know of a 3rd party email app which we can use without sending our data overseas?

posts.author: Yuval.Polturak (Yuval Polturak)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Josh @waterknot — totally feel your pain. A lot of folks hit the limits of Zapier for Klaviyo ↔︎ monday.com (tests pass, live runs don’t). We’re building a native monday.com app for Klaviyo and would love to make sure it covers real-world workflows.

If you’re open, could you share:

  • What should trigger from monday.com (item created/updated, status change, subitems)?

  • What you want to do in Klaviyo (create/update profile, add/remove from list, trigger a flow, record a custom event)?

  • Which fields need to map both ways?

Happy to offer early access. DM me here or email yuval@fantasymedia.io.

— Yuval, Fantasy Media

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @PayWay369 ,

I’ve noticed your comment and decided to answer, since our solution was developed exactly for your case.

The Board Email Reports app provides the following types of reports.
On a board/project level:

  • New and updated tasks
  • Status updates
  • Budget, Costs, Revenue changes
  • Timeline and deadline overdue
  • Time Tracking changes

On an item/task level (item updates section):

  • Updates by a specific employee
  • Updates mentioning a specific employee
  • Updates containing attachments
  • Status updates
  • Last updates

It tracks changes for the column and time period you set in the automation rule and sends an XLS report to the people you list (no guest access required!).

For example, this is an overdue items report:

image788×635 26.8 KB

image-2025-8-7_14-22-31258×224 28.9 KB

You can install the app and test it for free and find out if it works for you. We can make a custom automation rule for your team, just tell your request to our team.
